reasoning: The question asks if Vaishali R's official Elo rating (according to the Wikipedia FIDE Rankings page) will be functionally 1% higher on Nov 2, Nov 25, and Jan 24 than on Oct 26. A 1% increase over her known baseline of 2473 amounts to nearly +25 rating points (requiring a rating of >= 2498). For the first two resolution dates, the November 2025 rating list will apply. Since the cut-off for the Nov list is late October, she would have needed a massive +25 performance purely within the month of October. My searches did not reveal any active October classical tournaments for her, making a +25 gain basically nil for Nov 2 and Nov 25. For the January 2026 date, she has an additional two months of play to hit 2498. While mathematically possible given her high volatility (historically gaining/losing 20+ points in a single month at events like the Grand Swiss), it is still statistically unlikely. Therefore, uncertainty increases slightly over the longer time horizon but is still heavily weighted towards NO.
